name: Argentina (civil flag, 1818–1985)

colors.symbolism: the clear sky over Buenos Aires; the Bourbon dynastic colour adopted by the May Revolution
the clouds; purity; the silver of the Río de la Plata

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — light blue, white, light blue — with no Sun of May. The plain triband (the bandera de ornato) served as Argentina's civil flag from 1818 until civil and state versions were unified in 1985.
history: When the Sun of May was added to Argentina's flag on 25 February 1818, the official (state) flag bore the sun while a plain triband without the sun continued to fly as the civil flag for use by ordinary citizens and on civil buildings. This dual-flag arrangement persisted for more than 160 years. Decree 10,302 of 1944 codified the distinction. On 12 August 1985, Law 23,208 (under President Raúl Alfonsín) suppressed the bandera de ornato and provided that only the sun-bearing version (bandera oficial) should be used for both civil and state purposes. Since then the plain triband has no official role.

features.symbolism: the celestial blue and white of the cockade adopted by Manuel Belgrano in 1812; the colours of the Bourbon dynasty and of the cockades worn by the May 1810 revolutionaries
